name: Alija del Infantado and Castro de
name_original: Alija del Infantado a Castro de las Labradas — circular
description: This moderate 16.3-mile mountain bike route from Infantado to Castro de las Labradas offers a circular ride with historical views.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Alija Del Infantado offers diverse terrain within the province of León, Spain. The region is characterized by fertile plains, known as a "Vega," with rich cultivation areas and green meadows. Routes often traverse historical landscapes and rural scenery, providing a mix of paved and unpaved segments. This area provides a well-developed network for cycling, suitable for various skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Alija Del Infantado The most popular mountain bike trail is Castro de las Labradas Loop from Brime de Urz, a 24.0 miles (38.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 28 minutes to complete. This moderate route includes both paved and unpaved segments, catering to those seeking a rigorous experience. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Alija del Infantado and Castro de las Labradas, a moderate 16.3 miles (26.3 km) path. This route journeys through historical and rural scenery, offering a blend of natural beauty and cultural context. Local mountain bikers also love the Castillo de los Pimentel – La Bañeza Railway Station loop from La Bañeza, a 34.3 miles (55.2 km) trail leading through historical landmarks like the ruined Palace-Castle of the Pimentel family, often completed in about 3 hours 40 minutes. Mountain biking around Alija Del Infantado is defined by fertile plains, historical landscapes, and rural scenery. The network offers options for different ability levels, with all 5 routes in this guide rated as moderate. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 20 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Alija Del Infantado's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Alija Del Infantado?
A: There are 5 mountain bike trails detailed in this guide for the Alija Del Infantado area. All of them are rated as moderate, offering a good challenge for most riders.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Alija Del Infantado?
A: The trails around Alija Del Infantado traverse diverse terrain, including fertile plains, historical landscapes, and rural scenery. You'll encounter a mix of paved and unpaved segments, characteristic of the region's 'Vega' (cultivation areas and green meadows).

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1233718353/castro-de-las-labradas-loop-from-brime-de-urz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de las Labradas Loop from Brime de Urz&lt;/a&gt; is a 38.6 km loop, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24311085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bodegas de Zorrilla – Basilia's Balcony loop from Morales de Rey&lt;/a&gt; offers a 42.35 km circular ride.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see while mountain biking near Alija Del Infantado?
A: Many routes pass by significant historical sites. You can explore the area around the ruined &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5786385" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de los Pimentel&lt;/a&gt;, which is featured on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38162590" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de los Pimentel – La Bañeza Railway Station loop from La Bañeza&lt;/a&gt;. The region also has Roman vestiges and historical churches.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Alija Del Infantado?
A: While all routes in this guide are rated moderate, the region offers diverse landscapes. For a less strenuous family experience, consider exploring parts of the rural scenery or the 'Magical Forest Route' (Ruta del Bosque Mágico), a 2 km trail known for its imaginative decorations, though primarily a hiking trail, it suggests areas with gentler terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Alija Del Infantado?
A: The broader Castilla y León region, including Alija Del Infantado, generally offers good conditions for mountain biking during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be hot, and winter might bring colder weather, though specific trail conditions vary.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, some routes offer substantial distances. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38162347" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor of La Bañeza – Church of Santa Elena de Jamuz loop from La Bañeza&lt;/a&gt; is one of the longer options at 55.75 km, typically taking around 4 hours 43 minutes to complete.

Q: What natural features or viewpoints can I expect on the trails?
A: The area is characterized by fertile plains and green meadows. You might also encounter natural spaces with geodetic vertices, such as the one at Peña Mortero, which contains a petroglyph. The approach to the area from Puerto de Pajares also offers scenic mountain views.

Q: Are there any specific bridges or historical ruins to look out for?
A: Yes, you can find several interesting structures. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4092040" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de la Vizana&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5470465" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eria Bridge in Castrocalbón&lt;/a&gt; are notable bridges. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6352862" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of the Royal Monastery of Santa María de Nogales&lt;/a&gt; also offer a glimpse into the region's past.

Q: What is the typical difficulty level of mountain bike trails in Alija Del Infantado?
A: All 5 mountain bike routes featured in this guide are rated as moderate. This means they offer a good balance of challenge and accessibility, suitable for riders with some experience.

Q: Can I find accommodation or places to eat that are bike-friendly in Alija Del Infantado?
A: Alija Del Infantado is a town with historical features, including a Plaza Mayor and Town Hall, suggesting local amenities. While specific bike-friendly establishments aren't detailed, the presence of a town center (&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5786425" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alija del Infantado Town Center&lt;/a&gt;) indicates you'll likely find cafes, pubs, and accommodation options suitable for visitors.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Alija Del Infantado?
A: More than 20 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Alija Del Infantado's varied terrain. Riders often appreciate the blend of historical and rural scenery, as well as the well-developed network of trails that cater to different ability levels.
tips: Fortification of the town of Alija del Infantado dating from the 11th century, although it has been successively reformed by the different families to which it has belonged.

Ruins of a fortified settlement from the time of the Celts who fought the Romans when they arrived in this area of the Iberian Peninsula. The Castro de las Labradas is located on a steep hill and you will have to bike uphill or push.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the mountain bike route from Infantado to Castro de las Labradas?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for this exact route are not readily available, Infantado (also known as Alija del Infantado) is the starting point. You would typically look for public parking within the village to begin your ride. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain in the wider Zamora province, where this route is located, can be diverse. Expect a mix of wide paths and potentially less traveled routes. Some segments might be unpaved and could be rocky or challenging, requiring good fitness and mountain biking experience. The region features soft, rounded mountain reliefs and natural landscapes.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route features two significant highlights: the Castillo de los Pimentel, a historical castle, and Castro de las Labradas, an archaeological site. Castro de las Labradas is a fortified town with remains from the Neolithic and Iron Age Asturian era, offering historical insights and scenic views from its elevated position in the Sierra de Capurias.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: Mountain biking in Spain, including the Zamora province, can generally be enjoyed almost year-round due to the favorable climate. However, for the most comfortable experience, spring and autumn typically offer milder temperatures and pleasant conditions. Summers can be hot, and winters might bring colder weather, though snow is less common in lower elevations.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, trails in natural areas of Spain are dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock, and to clean up after them. Given the route's moderate difficulty and potential for varied terrain, ensure your dog is fit enough for the distance and conditions.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: There is no indication from available information that specific permits or entrance fees are required for mountain biking this route. Access to natural trails in Spain is generally free. However, always respect private property and any local regulations you may encounter.

faqs.question: Is the route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as 'moderate' with a distance of approximately 26 km and over 440 meters of elevation gain. While the Zamora province offers diverse trails, some segments on routes like this can be unpaved and difficult. It might be challenging for beginners or young children. Riders should have a good level of fitness and some mountain biking experience.

faqs.question: Are there any public transport options to reach the trailhead in Infantado?
faqs.answer: Specific public transport links directly to Infantado (Alija del Infantado) for trail access are not detailed. In rural areas of Spain, public transport can be limited. It's often more convenient to reach the starting point by car. Checking local bus schedules for the Zamora province would be necessary for precise information.

faqs.question: Does this mountain bike route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of the official trails such as the Mtb Vía de la Plata, Vía de la Plata hiking - 03 Salamanca to Astorga, Calle las Eras, Subida al castro de Arrabalde, Calle del Cristo, Camino A-5, and Camino A-8.

faqs.question: What kind of natural features or wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The region, particularly areas like the Sierra de la Culebra nearby, is known for its wild landscapes and extensive natural spaces. You might encounter a mix of reforested conifer masses, cork oak forests, and chestnut trees. The area also offers opportunities for wildlife sightings, though specific species are not guaranteed on this particular route.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the start or end of the route?
faqs.answer: Infantado (Alija del Infantado) is a village, so you can typically find local cafes or restaurants there to refuel before or after your ride. For options directly on the route, it's best to carry sufficient water and snacks, as services might be sparse between Infantado and Castro de las Labradas.
